channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life - Fox News
item.description:
1. 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life  Fox News
  2. For the First Time, a Cell Built From Scratch Grows and Divides  Quanta Magazine
  3. Scientists Say They've Made Cells That Feed, Grow and Reproduce, Bringing Them One Step Closer to Building Life From Scratch  Smithsonian Magazine
  4. What is 'SpudCell'? Arguably the greatest bioengineering feat yet  New Scientist
  5. Scientists take another step towards lab-made life  The Economist

item.title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star - Space
item.description:
1. 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star  Space

  3. ‘Bizarre’ planet orbiting dead star may preview fate of our solar system  CNN
  4. The sun’s violent death could look like this  Popular Science
  5. How a giant planet survived its star's death, then migrated inward  Phys.org

item.title: The growing number of satellites in orbit could soon make telescopes obsolete. 'For astronomy, this would obviously be catastrophic' - Space
item.description:

  4. ESO Study Finds That No More Than 100,000 Satellites Should Orbit Earth  Gizmodo

item.title: A Sea Anemone Uses CARDIB to Fight Viruses—and It’s Even More Bizarre Than It Sounds - Gizmodo

item.title: A tiny “quasi-moon” called Kamoʻoalewa shadows Earth’s orbit and may be a piece of the actual Moon blasted into space by an ancient impact — and China’s Tianwen-2 spacecraft is now closing in to bring a sample back to Earth. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A tiny “quasi-moon” called Kamoʻoalewa shadows Earth’s orbit and may be a piece of the actual Moon blasted into space by an ancient impact — and China’s Tianwen-2 spacecraft is now closing in to bring a sample back to Earth.  Space Daily
  2. China's Tianwen-2 Probe Has Likely Reached Earth's "Quasi-moon" Kamo‘oalewa. Now The Fun Begins  IFLScience
  3. China’s Tianwen-2 mission has (probably) arrived at a quasi-moon of Earth  The Planetary Society
  4. China Is About To Land On Earth’s ‘Mini-Moon’ — What To Know  Forbes
  5. Could this asteroid be a piece of the Moon? A Chinese spacecraft is about to find out  The Conversation

item.title: A galaxy confirmed this January formed just 290 million years after the Big Bang, and the part astronomers cannot explain is that it is far too large, far too soon, as if the early universe was building things it was not supposed to be able to build yet. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A galaxy confirmed this January formed just 290 million years after the Big Bang, and the part astronomers cannot explain is that it is far too large, far too soon, as if the early universe was building things it was not supposed to be able to build yet.  Space Daily
  2. The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw  Space
  3. The most distant lensing cluster reveals a dark matter surprise  Big Think
  4. James Webb Space Telescope catches 6 galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe  Yahoo
  5. The JWST Spies Six Galaxies Becoming One  Universe Today

  5. “Almost Impossible To Probe” Black Hole Event Horizon Might Have Shaped Loudest Gravitational Wave Signal  IFLScience

item.title: Demographers estimate that about 117 billion members of our species have ever been born, which means the roughly 8 billion of us alive today are about 7% of everyone who has ever lived - Space Daily

item.title: In 2024, researchers reported something that should have been impossible: metallic nodules scattered across the deep Pacific seafloor appeared to be producing oxygen in total darkness, creating “dark oxygen” without a single ray of sunlight. - Space Daily
item.description: In 2024, researchers reported something that should have been impossible: metallic nodules scattered across the deep Pacific seafloor appeared to be producing oxygen in total darkness, creating “dark oxygen” without a single ray of sunlight.  Space Daily

item.title: In 2024, engineers rescued Voyager 1 after it spent months transmitting gibberish, remotely rewriting and relocating pieces of 46-year-old code around a failed memory chip from more than 24 billion kilometres away. - Space Daily
item.description: In 2024, engineers rescued Voyager 1 after it spent months transmitting gibberish, remotely rewriting and relocating pieces of 46-year-old code around a failed memory chip from more than 24 billion kilometres away.  Space Daily

item.title: Study suggests life on Earth has around 1.8 billion years left — but the biosphere might evolve to survive... - Yahoo
item.description:

  2. Study suggests life on Earth has around 1.8 billion years left — but the biosphere might evolve to survive even longer  Live Science
  3. Study predicts when the last plants on Earth will die  New Atlas
  4. How long can plants survive on Earth? New model suggests up to 2 billion more years  Phys.org
  5. How Long Will Life on Earth Last? A New Calculation Extends the Timeline to 1.8 Billion Years or More if We Can Modify the Planet’s Orbit  La Brújula Verde
